element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns core jump and attack mechanics with basic monsters
Player must return to household to swap characters and obtain passwords
Monster drops provide keys; player learns resource collection

element_introduction_order.element: Magic system and magic power depletion
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Attacks consume magic; potions restore it
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource management becomes critical as areas demand sustained combat

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Found in chests or purchased in shops
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Wings, mattock, magical gloves, magical key unlock specific dungeon sections
Bricks that require mattock or gloves; environmental puzzles gate progression

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Four major dungeon sections (one per crown)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each boss guards a crown; requires optimal character and item selection
Four crowns accumulate; only Roas can use them to find Dragon Slayer

element_introduction_order.element: Dragon Slayer sword and final boss
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Fifth dungeon section after all crowns obtained
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Only Roas wields it; used to defeat Keela in final confrontation

typical_relief_triggers: Finding shops or inns for healing/supplies
Obtaining magic power and health restoration items
typical_tension_triggers: Low magic power forcing character swaps
Encountering new enemy types in unexplored sections
Navigating sections designed for characters without optimal items
Extensive monster-filled passages requiring sustained combat
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical with mandatory breaks for character selection; tension escalates through dungeon depth and section-specific boss encounters; relief provided by safe household returns and shop access

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Shops in impractical locations suggest hidden areas and optional exploration; chests scattered throughout contain magical items
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Magical items hidden in chests; specific character abilities unlock hidden paths (wings enable flight, mattock breaks special bricks, gloves move blocks)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to character switching and basic combat; simple monster encounters; straightforward key/door progression
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Character-specific puzzles emerge; magic management becomes critical; varied enemy types; environmental hazards increase; boss encounters begin
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multi-stage puzzles requiring specific character combinations; sustained resource management; four boss gauntlet; final boss demands mastery of Roas and Dragon Slayer mechanics
notable_spikes: Each of the four crown sections (designed with specific character in mind)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of five major dungeon sections has noticeably different characteristics and background music; designed with one playable character's strengths in mind; crowns cannot be acquired without proper character selection
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Section requiring mattock (Lyll) vs. section requiring magical gloves (Xemn)

notable_level_patterns.description: Progression blocked by locked doors requiring keys from monster drops; shops in inconvenient locations require deliberate exploration; items scattered in chests force thorough dungeon mapping
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Magical items accessible only by purchasing or finding in distant chests

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must return to household to swap characters and obtain passwords for continued play; this creates natural relief breaks and forces strategic team planning
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Household serves as hub; player must plan which character to deploy before each dungeon excursion

notable_level_patterns.description: Four distinct dungeon sections each contain one boss guarding a crown; fifth section contains final boss; geometry and enemy placement unique per section
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Crown 1-4 boss encounters; Keela final battle
